vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1027

plan.title: Coverage threshold blind spot: pygame/tkinter event loops can't run headlessly, dragging overall % below 85% threshold

narratives.Description: The deft 85% coverage threshold assumes logic-heavy code, so a project whose entry point runs a pygame or tkinter event loop cannot execute headlessly and reports an inflated per-session coverage that collapses when the full src/ is measured. The fix teaches the setup skill and the Python language guide to flag display-dependent modules and the headless-test pattern so the blind spot is caught during scaffolding instead of at the check gate.
narratives.Overview:
## Summary
The deft 85% coverage threshold assumption fails for projects where the primary source file contains pygame/tkinter/Electron event loops that cannot execute without a display. This causes a silent false-pass during agent implementation (agent reports high coverage) followed by a check failure when the full src/ is measured.

## Observed impact
During a desktop-UI swarm agent run:
- Agent reported 97% coverage (measured only files it had imported in its test session)
- After the fix commit, full 	ask check showed 69% — src/ui.py at 43% dragged the total below threshold
- Required adding src/ui.py to [tool.coverage.run] omit in pyproject.toml as a workaround

The 85% threshold is correct for logic-heavy code but creates friction for any project whose project_type is Desktop App or TUI.

## Proposed fix
One or more of:
1. The deft-directive-setup Phase 2 output for project_type = Desktop App should scaffold pyproject.toml with a coverage.run.omit entry for src/ui.py (or equivalent display-dependent entry point)
2. The Python language file (languages/python.md) should document the headless-test pattern: os.environ.setdefault("SDL_VIDEODRIVER", "dummy") and coverage omit for event-loop modules
3. The setup skill should warn when project_type = Desktop App: "pygame/tkinter event loops cannot be tested headlessly — consider excluding UI entry points from coverage measurement"

## Source
Discovered during tic-tac-toe desktop-UI swarm agent (2026-05-10). pyproject.toml patched mid-session.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: Update the skills/deft-directive-setup/SKILL.md file so the Phase 2 Desktop App path warns about display-dependent event loops and suggests a coverage omit entry for the UI entry-point module. Update languages/python.md to record the headless-test pattern, including the SDL_VIDEODRIVER=dummy environment setting and a coverage.run omit example for event-loop source files. Reference the desktop-UI swarm recurrence so the guidance ties back to a concrete failure and its test evidence.
narratives.UserStory: As a deft user scaffolding a desktop-UI project, I want the setup skill and Python guide to cover headless coverage handling, so that display-dependent event-loop modules do not silently drag coverage below the threshold.

items.title: Setup skill warns about headless coverage for Desktop App projects.

narrative.Acceptance: Given project_type is Desktop App, when the setup skill runs Phase 2, then it emits a warning that pygame/tkinter event loops cannot be tested headlessly and suggests a coverage omit for the UI entry point.

items.title: python.md records the headless-test pattern and a coverage omit example.

narrative.Acceptance: When languages/python.md is read, then it records the SDL_VIDEODRIVER=dummy headless-test pattern and a coverage.run omit example for event-loop modules.

items.title: Applying the omit lets task check pass on a desktop-UI project.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a desktop-UI project, when the recommended coverage omit is applied, then task check measures only logic modules and no longer fails on the display-dependent entry point.

references.title: Issue #1027: Coverage threshold blind spot: pygame/tkinter event loops can't run headlessly, dragging overall % below 85% threshold

expected_outputs: setup skill warns about headless coverage for Desktop App projects
python.md documents the SDL_VIDEODRIVER headless-test pattern
coverage omit guidance for event-loop entry points
